steps.title: Identify the central trade-off
steps.content:
The passage establishes that RSRs protect existing policyholders but result in “reducing the competitive pressure that typically restrains premium prices for new policies.” This phrase is the key: it predicts that new-policy prices will rise because competition will be weaker.

The blank must capture the split benefit—helping current customers while harming those seeking new policies.

steps.title: Match the outcome to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly captures the outcome: RSRs benefit existing policyholders at the expense of nonpolicyholders seeking new policies.

The other three miss the split—either reversing the pricing outcome (C), misidentifying the winner (D), or introducing unsupported consequences (B).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
